description: Ground-truth relevance judgments for RAG method comparison. Each query maps to a set of chunk IDs that contain relevant supporting information. Designed for MachineLearningNotes.pdf (CampusX 100 Days of ML).

what is gradient descent.note: Chunks discussing iterative optimization, cost function minimization, learning rate, and gradient steps. Auto-matched by keyword intersection with chunk text.

explain the bias variance tradeoff.note: Chunks covering model complexity, underfitting, overfitting, bias, variance, and the tradeoff between them.

how does a decision tree work.note: Chunks explaining entropy, information gain, Gini impurity, splitting criteria, and tree construction.

what is regularization in machine learni.note: Chunks discussing L1/L2 regularization, ridge, lasso, weight decay, and preventing overfitting.

explain support vector machines.note: Chunks covering margin maximization, kernel trick, support vectors, and hyperplane separation.

what is cross validation.note: Chunks explaining k-fold, LOOCV, validation strategy, and model selection.

how does logistic regression work.note: Chunks covering sigmoid function, log odds, maximum likelihood, and binary classification.

what is principal component analysis.note: Chunks explaining dimensionality reduction, eigenvalues, eigenvectors, and variance explained.

explain the concept of overfitting.note: Chunks discussing training vs test error, model complexity, generalization gap, and solutions.

what is the difference between supervise.note: Chunks covering labeled vs unlabeled data, classification, clustering, and learning paradigms.
